steps.title: Find the constant ratio
steps.content:
The ratio of $k$ to $p$ is constant. Using $k=5$ and $p=4$ gives
$$\frac{k}{p}=\frac{5}{4}$$

steps.title: Apply the ratio to the new value
steps.content:
When $p=28$, multiply by the constant ratio:
$$k=\frac{5}{4}\cdot 28=5\cdot 7=35$$The answer is 35.
